summary: This week was the site itself: chrisscho.uk went from a single-page portfolio to a multi-page system with live data. The fake terminal and status chips were replaced with a real GitHub commit feed and genuine uptime pings, and the pipeline that writes this very log was built and wired up.

highlights.text: Replaced hardcoded status chips with real server-side uptime pings (HEAD requests, measured latency, honest failure states).
Hero terminal now replays real commits from the GitHub API with a checked-in snapshot fallback.
Added projects, services, and writing sections with full SEO infrastructure: sitemap, robots, JSON-LD, dynamic OG images.
Cut public image payload from ~13MB to ~400KB via a sharp optimization script.
Shipped this build-log pipeline: a weekly GitHub Action that summarizes org commits via the Claude API and commits the entry back.

generatedBy: seed entry, written by hand during the site rebuild — future entries are generated by claude-haiku-4-5 via GitHub Actions
